nodes.label: backfill_temporal_triples.py
nodes.description: One-time migration to backfill temporal triples for v1.13.0
nodes.summary: Generates occurred_on and has_source triples for all existing working_memory and episodic_memory entries that lack them. Supports --dry-run and --db-path flags.

nodes.label: Temporal Triples Migration
nodes.description: One-time migration script for Mnemosyne v1.13.0. Backfills occurred_on and has_source triples into legacy triples table. Post-E6, these belong in annotations table with auto-migrate hook.

nodes.label: cleanup_noisy_mentions.py
nodes.description: Remove noisy mention annotations leaked before entity stopword fix
nodes.summary: Removes 26 known noise words from annotations table kind='mentions'. Creates backup before modifying. Supports --dry-run and --db flags.

nodes.label: NOISE_WORDS
nodes.description: 26 noise words removed: ASSISTANT, USER, SKILL, Review, Target, CLASS, LEVEL, Signals, Phase, API, Summary, Added, Active, Be, Not, Whether, All, No, Replying, AI, Memory, False, True, None, Signal, Hermes, Agent, Model, System, Fact, Mnemosyne.

nodes.label: Noisy Mention Cleanup
nodes.description: Removes meta-system noise words from annotations table that leaked in before the entity extraction stopword fix (PR #120). Creates timestamped backup first.

nodes.label: heal_quality.py
nodes.description: Self-healing LLM-as-Judge pipeline for degraded episodic entries
nodes.summary: Detects degraded entries (bullet-format, <300 chars) and repairs via 4-stage LLM loop: Extract memory units from session JSONs -> Generate summary with M2.7/local LLM -> Judge across 4 dimensions -> Repair with fault-specific strategies. Supports MiniMax M2.7 or local GGUF.

nodes.label: LLM-as-Judge Quality Pipeline
nodes.description: 4-stage pipeline: Extract memory units from session files -> Generate summary via M2.7/local LLM -> Judge across 4 dimensions (factual density, format compliance, length sufficiency, grounding) -> Repair with fault-specific strategies (truncated, generic, missing_facts, wrong_format). Retry loop with M2.7 escalation.

nodes.label: Quality Judge 4 Dimensions
nodes.description: 1. FACTUAL_DENSITY (names, paths, versions). 2. FORMAT_COMPLIANCE (plain prose, no bullets). 3. LENGTH_SUFFICIENCY (>300 chars). 4. GROUNDING (matches source memories). Each scored 0-100.

nodes.label: Env Config for Heal Quality
nodes.description: Config knobs: MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_JUDGE_THRESHOLD=75, MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_MAX_RETRIES=3, MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_MIN_LEN=300, MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_BUDGET=4000, MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_ESCALATE_AFTER=2, MNEMOSYNE_HEAL_JUDGE_MODEL=MiniMax-M2.7

nodes.label: memoria_audit.py
nodes.description: Health audit tool for 5 MEMORIA tables with noise classification
nodes.summary: Audits 5 MEMORIA tables: memoria_facts, memoria_instructions, memoria_preferences, memoria_timelines, memoria_kg. Samples 100 entries per table, classifies each as useful/noise/ambiguous, generates summary with ASCII bar charts.

nodes.label: 5 MEMORIA Tables
nodes.description: memoria_facts (key-value facts), memoria_instructions (imperatives/constraints), memoria_preferences (user preferences), memoria_timelines (temporal events), memoria_kg (knowledge graph triples).

nodes.label: Noise Classification Rules
nodes.description: Facts: noise_weather, noise_fragment, noise_system_metric, noise_low_importance, noise_date_mention. Instructions: noise_false_positive, noise_should_conversational. Preferences: noise_informational, noise_vague_want. Timelines: noise_filepath, noise_no_event_context. KG: noise_negation_fragment, noise_decision_fragment.

nodes.label: migrate_from_legacy.py
nodes.description: Migrate memories from ephemeral/legacy DBs to canonical persisted path
nodes.summary: Migrates from ~/.mnemosyne/data/ (ephemeral, lost on Fly.io restart) to ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/data/ (persisted). Copies memories, embeddings; promotes meaningful entries to episodic_memory and top 30 high-importance to working_memory. Supports --purge-tools.

nodes.label: Canonical DB Path
nodes.description: Canonical path: ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/data/mnemosyne.db (persisted across restarts). Legacy: ~/.mnemosyne/data/mnemosyne.db (ephemeral on Fly.io).

nodes.label: Legacy DB Candidates
nodes.description: 3 legacy paths scanned: ~/.mnemosyne/data/mnemosyne.db, ~/.mnemosyne/data/mnemosyne_native.db, ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/data/mnemosyne_native.db

nodes.label: mnemosyne-stats.py
nodes.description: Terminal-based BEAM health dashboard with snapshot/trend tracking
nodes.summary: Full health dashboard reading directly from SQLite. Shows: working memory breakdown (source, importance, recall distributions), episodic stats, KG triples, annotations, consolidation log, Dreamer runs, embeddings, wiki status, top recalled, memory age, session activity. Quality scoring (A-F grade), trend tracking via snapshots.

nodes.label: Health Dashboard Quality Score
nodes.description: 7-point quality score: noise<30%, recall>30%, global>5%, embeddings>0, consolidation>0, dreamer>0, wiki>10 pages. Grade: A(6-7), B(5), C(4), D(3), F(0-2).

nodes.label: Stats Snapshot and Trend Tracking
nodes.description: Timestamped JSON snapshots saved to ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/stats/snap_*.json. Trend tracking compares last 2 valid snapshots with delta and % change for 12 metrics.

nodes.label: setup.py
nodes.description: Package build configuration for mnemosyne-memory
nodes.summary: Standard setup.py with setuptools. Package name: mnemosyne-memory. Author: Abdias J. MIT license. Python >=3.10. Optional deps: llm (ctransformers, llama-cpp-python), embeddings (fastembed), mcp, openclaw, all. Console scripts: mnemosyne-install, mnemosyne-uninstall, mnemosyne, mnemosyne-browser, mnemosyne-auto-save.

nodes.label: Package Keywords
nodes.description: ai, memory, sqlite, agent, llm, context, embeddings, vector-store, honcho, zep, mcp, openwebui, openclaw, cursor, claude-code, codex, hermes, model-context-protocol

nodes.label: Console Scripts
nodes.description: mnemosyne-install -> install(), mnemosyne-uninstall -> uninstall(), mnemosyne -> mnemosyne.cli:run_cli, mnemosyne-browser -> memory_browser:main, mnemosyne-auto-save -> auto_save_openwebui:main

nodes.label: Extra Require Groups
nodes.description: llm (ctransformers+llama-cpp-python+huggingface-hub), embeddings (fastembed), mcp (mcp+anyio), openclaw, all (combined), dev (pytest+build+twine)

nodes.label: mnemosyne-memory-override.md
nodes.description: Hard rule override forcing Mnemosyne for durable memory storage
nodes.summary: Hermes Agent skill document that overrides the legacy memory tool with Mnemosyne for durable facts. Provides mapping table (old memory -> new mnemosyne_remember). Defines when legacy memory is acceptable (ephemeral session state only). Migration cleanup procedure: save to Mnemosyne, then remove from legacy.

nodes.label: Mnemosyne Memory Override Rule
nodes.description: CRITICAL: Never use legacy 'memory' tool for durable facts. Use mnemosyne_remember() instead. Legacy has 2,200 char limit and no vector search. Exceptions: ephemeral session state, when Mnemosyne is confirmed down.

nodes.label: conftest.py
nodes.description: Shared test fixtures with SQLite connection cleanup and LLM auto-disable
nodes.summary: Pytest fixtures: autouse connection resetter for SQLite thread-local caches, autouse local LLM disabler to prevent real CPU inference during tests (reduces suite from 15+ mins to ~20s). local_llm_enabled fixture for tests needing fake LLM with dual ctransformers/llama-cpp-python interface.

nodes.label: _reset_thread_local_connections
nodes.description: Autouse fixture that closes SQLite connection caches in beam, memory, hermes_plugin, and llm_backends modules before and after every test.

nodes.label: _disable_local_llm_inference
nodes.description: Autouse fixture that monkeypatches _load_llm to return None and clears cached LLM state, preventing real CPU inference from auto-loaded GGUF models.

nodes.label: local_llm_enabled
nodes.description: Opt-in fixture providing a fake LLM with both __call__ (ctransformers) and create_chat_completion (llama-cpp-python) interfaces. Configurable response.

nodes.label: test_benchmark_preflight.py
nodes.description: Preflight regression tests for BEAM benchmark harness
nodes.summary: Tests that evaluate_beam_end_to_end.py refuses to run with harness oracles active unless --pure-recall or --allow-harness-oracles is set. 7 tests covering CLI flags, env vars, and preflight ordering before dataset load.

nodes.label: TestPreflightRefusesWithoutPureRecall
nodes.description: 7 test methods verifying preflight gate in benchmark harness.

nodes.label: Benchmark Harness Preflight Gate
nodes.description: Harness refuses unless MNEMOSYNE_BENCHMARK_PURE_RECALL=1 (or --pure-recall) or --allow-harness-oracles. Prevents oracle-contaminated arm comparisons. Fails before dataset load with exit code 2.

nodes.label: test_cli_errors.py
nodes.description: CLI error handling regression tests
nodes.summary: Tests CLI error handling for: invalid importance values, import missing file, JSON validation, export/import roundtrip counts, bank CRUD operations. Verifies clean error messages without Python tracebacks. 7 test functions.

nodes.label: CLI Error Test Cases
nodes.description: store(importance not float), recall(top_k not int), update(importance not float), import(missing file). Each expects non-zero exit, clear error message, no traceback.

nodes.label: test_cli_operation_failures.py
nodes.description: CLI operation failure regression tests (update/delete missing)
nodes.summary: Tests that update and delete commands on non-existent memory IDs return exit code 1 with 'Memory not found' error message.

nodes.label: test_cli_usage_errors.py
nodes.description: CLI usage error regression tests
nodes.summary: Tests that missing required args on 7 CLI commands (store, recall, update, delete, import, import-hindsight, bank) produce correct usage strings. Also tests unknown command error and --help exit.

nodes.label: CLI Usage Test Cases
nodes.description: 7 commands tested for correct usage strings: store, recall, update, delete, import, import-hindsight, bank.

nodes.label: CLI Error Message Hygiene
nodes.description: All CLI tests verify: non-zero exit code (1 or 2), clear user-facing error message in stderr, NO Python tracebacks in output. Consistent error behavior across 7+ CLI commands.

conftest.py.purpose: Prevent cross-test DB lock contamination and disable real CPU inference

backfill_temporal_triples.py.relationship: Both are one-time migration scripts for database evolution; backfill adds temporal triples, migrate moves data to canonical path
backfill_temporal_triples.py.shared_context: Database migration: temporal schema evolution and data relocation

cleanup_noisy_mentions.py.relationship: Both deal with data quality in annotation/MEMORIA tables; cleanup removes noise, audit classifies quality
cleanup_noisy_mentions.py.shared_context: Data quality: noise detection, removal, and classification

heal_quality.py.relationship: heal_quality repairs degraded episodic entries; memoria_audit classifies table quality; mnemosyne-stats shows overall health indicators that can trigger healing
heal_quality.py.shared_context: Memory quality pipeline: detection, classification, repair, and monitoring

migrate_from_legacy.py.relationship: Both migration scripts: migrate moves data to canonical path, backfill adds temporal triples on top

setup.py.shared_context: Package distribution and entry point registration

mnemosyne-memory-override.md.relationship: The skill enforces Mnemosyne usage for durable memory; install.py sets up Mnemosyne as the memory provider

conftest.py.relationship: conftest.py provides shared fixtures (SQLite cleanup, LLM disable) used by all test files in the tests/ directory
conftest.py.shared_context: Test infrastructure: shared fixtures and setup/teardown logic

backfill_temporal_triples.py.description: One-time migration to backfill temporal triples for v1.13.0
backfill_temporal_triples.py.purpose: Generate occurred_on and has_source triples for all existing working_memory and episodic_memory entries lacking them
keyFindings: Generates occurred_on and has_source triples into legacy triples table
Post-E6: rows belong in annotations table, auto-migrated on next BeamMemory() init
Batch processing with 500-row commit intervals
Non-interactive mode auto-proceeds
Idempotent: only processes entries without existing occurred_on triple
recommendations: Prefer running on pre-E6 database; on post-E6 the auto-migrate hook handles it

cleanup_noisy_mentions.py.description: Remove noisy mention annotations leaked before entity stopword fix
cleanup_noisy_mentions.py.purpose: Remove meta-system noise words from annotations table that leaked before PR #120 entity extraction stopword fix
keyFindings: Removes 26 known noise words from annotations kind='mentions'
Creates timestamped backup before modifying database
Shows breakdown by noise word and top 15 surviving mentions after cleanup
Supports dry-run mode for preview

heal_quality.py.description: Self-healing LLM-as-Judge pipeline for degraded episodic entries
heal_quality.py.purpose: Detect and repair degraded episodic memory entries via LLM-as-Judge loop
keyFindings: 4-stage pipeline: Extract -> Generate -> Judge -> Repair
Detects degraded entries: bullet-format <150 chars or <100 chars with few spaces
MiniMax M2.7 via mmx-cli as primary LLM, local GGUF as fallback
4 judge dimensions scored 0-100: factual density, format compliance, length sufficiency, grounding
Fault-specific repair strategies: truncated, generic, missing_facts, wrong_format, escalate to M2.7
Retry loop with configurable max retries (3) and M2.7 escalation after 2 retries
Session file extraction from ~/.hermes/sessions/ with 4K token budget
Metadata tracking: quality_score, fault_before_repair, retry_loop_count, needs_human_review
recommendations: Requires mmx-cli installed for M2.7 access; falls back gracefully

memoria_audit.py.description: Health audit tool for 5 MEMORIA tables with noise classification
memoria_audit.py.purpose: Audit the quality of MEMORIA table entries: classify as useful, noise, or ambiguous
keyFindings: Audits 5 tables: memoria_facts, memoria_instructions, memoria_preferences, memoria_timelines, memoria_kg
Samples 100 random entries per table with classification rules
Facts: checks weather noise, fragments, system metrics, date mentions
Instructions: detects false positives, imperative quality, technical 'should' usage
Preferences: filters informational wants vs real preferences
Timelines: identifies events vs file paths vs noise
KG triples: evaluates negation/decision coherence
ASCII bar chart visualization per category
recommendations: Run periodically to track MEMORIA data quality trends

migrate_from_legacy.py.description: Migrate memories from ephemeral/legacy DBs to canonical persisted path
migrate_from_legacy.py.purpose: Move memories from ~/.mnemosyne/ (ephemeral on Fly.io) to ~/.hermes/mnemosyne/ (persisted)
keyFindings: Critical for Fly.io/ephemeral VMs: only ~/.hermes persists across restarts
Ensures canonical DB schema with all tables: memories, memory_embeddings, working_memory, episodic_memory, scratchpad, FTS5
Copies missing memories and embeddings idempotently
Migrates meaningful non-tool memories into episodic_memory
Promotes top 30 high-importance memories into working_memory
Supports --purge-tools to remove legacy tool_execution memories
Scans 3 legacy paths for sources
recommendations: Run after deploying to Fly.io to ensure data persistence

mnemosyne-stats.py.description: Terminal-based BEAM health dashboard with snapshot/trend tracking
mnemosyne-stats.py.purpose: Provide comprehensive health overview of the Mnemosyne BEAM memory system
keyFindings: Zero dependencies (reads SQLite directly with sqlite3 stdlib)
Shows: working memory by source, importance distribution, recall distribution
Episodic memory: total, avg importance, recent entries preview
Knowledge graph: triples total, predicate breakdown
Consolidation: events, items, recent runs
Dreamer: runs, proposals, conflicts, recent runs with status
Embeddings, scratchpad, wiki page counts
Memory age distribution (today/this week/this month/older)
Session activity (last 7 days)
7-point quality grade: A through F with individual indicator checks
Actionable recommendations for detected issues
Snapshot/trend tracking with delta computation

setup.py.purpose: Define Python package metadata, dependencies, and entry points for mnemosyne-memory
keyFindings: Package: mnemosyne-memory, MIT license
Python >=3.10, production/stable classifier
6 optional dependency groups: llm, embeddings, mcp, openclaw, all, dev
5 console scripts: install, uninstall, CLI, browser, auto-save
18 keywords covering AI/memory ecosystem integrations
Version auto-read from mnemosyne/__init__.py

mnemosyne-memory-override.md.description: Hard rule override forcing Mnemosyne for durable memory storage
mnemosyne-memory-override.md.purpose: Override the agent's reflex to use legacy memory() tool; redirect all durable memory writes to mnemosyne_remember()
keyFindings: CRITICAL: Never use legacy memory tool for durable facts
4 mapping rules: add target=user -> mnemosyne_remember(importance=0.9+, source=preference)
Legacy memory has 2,200 char limit and no vector search
Legacy acceptable only for ephemeral session state
Migration procedure: save to Mnemosyne first, then remove from legacy
Self-enforcement: cancel, use correct tool, set importance>=0.7

conftest.py.description: Shared test fixtures with SQLite connection cleanup and LLM auto-disable
conftest.py.purpose: Provide clean, deterministic test environment by resetting shared state and disabling LLM inference
keyFindings: Autouse fixture resets thread-local SQLite connections in beam, memory, hermes_plugin, llm_backends
Resets global Mnemosyne default instance and hermes_plugin singletons
Autouse fixture disables local LLM model loader to prevent CPU inference in tests
Reduces test suite from 15+ minutes to ~20 seconds when GGUF model on disk
Opt-in local_llm_enabled fixture provides fake LLM with dual interface
Supports monkeypatch and unittest.mock override patterns
recommendations: Tests that need LLM should use local_llm_enabled fixture

test_benchmark_preflight.py.description: Preflight regression tests for BEAM benchmark harness
test_benchmark_preflight.py.purpose: Verify that the benchmark harness correctly refuses to run with active oracles unless explicitly allowed
keyFindings: 7 tests in TestPreflightRefusesWithoutPureRecall class
Tests CLI flags (--pure-recall, --allow-harness-oracles)
Tests env vars (MNEMOSYNE_BENCHMARK_PURE_RECALL)
Tests that preflight fails before dataset load
Uses subprocess to invoke harness in argument-parsing mode
Expected exit code 2 for refusal, clean error message in stderr

test_cli_errors.py.purpose: Verify CLI produces clean user-facing error messages for invalid inputs
keyFindings: Tests 4 input validation error cases
Tests import error cases: missing file, non-object JSON, malformed JSON
Tests export/import roundtrip with correct memory counts
Tests bank CRUD operations with validation
All tests verify: non-zero exit, error in stderr, NO tracebacks

test_cli_operation_failures.py.description: CLI operation failure regression tests (update/delete missing)
test_cli_operation_failures.py.purpose: Verify CLI reports clean 'Memory not found' error for non-existent memory IDs
keyFindings: Tests update and delete on missing memory IDs
Expected: exit code 1, empty stdout, 'Memory not found' in stderr
No Python tracebacks in output

test_cli_usage_errors.py.purpose: Verify CLI produces correct usage strings when required arguments are missing
keyFindings: Tests 7 CLI commands for correct usage strings
Tests unknown command with helpful error message
Tests --help exits successfully with usage info
All tests: non-zero exit, usage in stderr, empty stdout, no tracebacks
